title: 1.1: Tests that the Parse function decodes a simple hexadecimal ETag string into a byte slice
1.2: Tests that the Parse function handles double-quoted ETag strings by stripping the quotes
1.3: Tests that the Parse function correctly handles multipart ETag format with dash and part number suffix
1.4: Tests that the Parse function returns an error for malformed ETag strings
1.5: Tests that the Equal function returns true when comparing two identical parsed ETags
1.6: Tests that the Equal function returns true when comparing quoted and unquoted representations of the same ETag
1.7: Tests that the Equal function returns false when comparing different ETags
1.8: Tests that the Multipart function computes a combined ETag from multiple part ETags
1.9: Tests that the Multipart function result includes the part count suffix
1.10: Tests that the Multipart function excludes already-multipart ETags from the computation
1.11: Tests that the Multipart function excludes encrypted ETags from the computation
1.12: Tests that the IsEncrypted method returns true for ETags representing encrypted objects
1.13: Tests that the IsEncrypted method returns false for regular non-encrypted ETags
1.14: Tests that the Format method returns the ETag unchanged for regular non-encrypted ETags
1.15: Tests that the Format method extracts the trailing 16 bytes for encrypted ETags to produce AWS S3 compatible output
1.16: Tests that Multipart output matches the AWS S3 multipart ETag algorithm for an input containing at least two valid single-part ETags
1.17: Tests that Parse returns an error for an unterminated double-quoted ETag input
1.18: Tests that Parse returns an error for a multipart ETag whose part-count suffix contains a non-digit character
1.20: Tests that Parse returns an error for a long hex ETag input that includes a '-' multipart separator
1.22: Tests that Equal returns false when comparing a plain ETag '<hex>' to a multipart ETag '<hex>-<n>' with the same digest prefix
1.23: Tests that Multipart counts identical part ETags as separate parts when computing the resulting multipart ETag
1.24: Tests that IsEncrypted returns false for a multipart ETag with a large numeric part-count suffix
2.1: Tests added by the patch are located under internal/etag/ in a Go *_test.go file
3.1: All tests use the Go testing package with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the Go naming convention with Test prefix followed by descriptive name
3.3: Each newly added Test* function covers at least two distinct input cases or subcases
3.4: Newly added tests report failures using testing.T methods rather than panicking
4.1: The parsing-focused test validates at least one successful Parse result against an expected ETag value
